name: Kai
epithet: Making Amends at the Wrong Moment
bio: Kai entered the fray at his sister Elmarie's instruction: find Aleshin, a young martial arts master she had sent into Kryvstof before the quarantine, who had gone quiet. It was the kind of mission that could have been phrased as a second chance, an opportunity to do something right after years of doing things in service of a clan he had left. He understood it as that. He went.

He found the scorch marks. He found the evidence of a fight that had ended without Aleshin surviving it, the golden energy burned into walls that were still standing, the signature of someone who had gone valiant rather than quietly. He filed this away in the part of him that was good at holding things until there was room for them, and stayed useful, and stayed completely useful, all the way through the mission.

The grief and the failure sat together in him for a long time afterward — not as separate things but as a combined weight, the weight of trying to make amends and finding the timing had been wrong. He proved to be a valuable member of the crew, which was true and also wasn't the thing he had come to prove. His flaming fist burns through the ooze directly, which made him effective throughout and saved the lives of everyone around him multiple times. He knows this. It doesn't close the gap.
